authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. This official compliance guide is authoritative for live-event ticket price, fee, and add-on disclosure handling that shapes booking and refund-boundary coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Dependency intake and guest-facing pricing validation

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Ticket Sales
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the authoritative federal enforcer of ADA Title III public-accommodation requirements. This guidance is authoritative for accessible seating inventory, comparable pricing, sales-channel parity, and hold-release rules at arts and entertainment venues.
used_for: limits on unsafe accessible-seat routing assumptions

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Current Edition
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public baseline used by many U.S. jurisdictions for price verification procedures, making it authoritative for posted-price versus system-price evidence handling across tickets, fees, parking, and merchandise add-ons.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Booking, order, or payment mismatch review

authoritative_sources.title: Group Visits
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Smithsonian is a major public cultural operator. Its official group-visits guidance is an authoritative public operating reference for group reservation lead times, required request details, arrival logistics, and visit planning artifacts in a cultural-program context.

authoritative_sources.title: Entry and Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Smithsonian's official entry guidance is an authoritative public operating reference for visitor-entry controls, prohibited-item handling, and security-screening expectations that shape guest-impact and safety routing at cultural venues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety, access, and prohibited-item blocker escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Code of Ethics for Museums
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AAM is the recognized professional body for U.S. museums. Its Code of Ethics is a direct professional framework for public trust, public service, and access obligations that inform cultural-program guest-handling and boundary posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-team program coordination and public-service boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Better Online Ticket Sales Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal enforcer for the Better Online Ticket Sales Act. The statute is authoritative for access-control circumvention, automated ticket-buying abuse, and resale of improperly obtained tickets.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer exception handling with fraud-aware escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Protecting Personal Information: A Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is an authoritative public reference for safeguarding customer information and limiting unsafe exposure of guest, payment, and identity records in service and handoff workflows.
used_for: limits on exposing personal or payment data

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized cross-brand standards body for payment-card security. PCI DSS is authoritative for merchants and venues that store, process, or transmit cardholder data, constraining payment-record handling and loss-prevention escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Booking, order, or payment mismatch review

authoritative_sources.title: Venue Guide for Security Enhancements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the federal risk-management agency for commercial facilities. Its venue-security guidance is authoritative public direction for suspicious-activity response, screening posture, and escalation boundaries at public gathering spaces.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety, access, and prohibited-item blocker escalation

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and guest-facing pricing or access validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect the request, program identifier, prior owner, booking or reservation records, ticket or roster status, accessibility context, and destination-owner candidate before any handoff proceeds.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team program coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Normalize program-calendar, run-of-show, room or seat assignment, group-arrival, guest-service, and service-recovery evidence; confirm owner transitions; and prepare handoff outputs with explicit artifact requirements.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Booking, order, or payment mismatch review
workflow_stages.description: Preserve booking, payment, fee, promotion, and membership conflicts as evidence-backed mismatches and route them without unsupported refund, pricing, or chargeback authority.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Safety, access, and prohibited-item blocker escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate crowd-safety, prohibited-item, suspicious-ticketing, identity, or public-entry blockers so the lane does not overstate security or fraud authority.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: High-volume intake or service execution
workflow_stages.description: Keep holiday weekends, sold-out performances, timed-entry surges, festival queues, and workshop check-ins moving with queue segmentation by authoritative record readiness and blocker state rather than by unsupported manual override.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Mark transfer complete only when the destination owner, required records, and blocker resolution are explicit; otherwise return safely for escalation or missing-tenant-fact handling.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: program calendars, run-of-show revisions, room or seat assignment records, and timing windows
ticket orders, reservations, group sales records, timed-entry bookings, rosters, and will-call lists
guest or customer records, accessibility accommodation requests, service tickets, and communication logs
POS transactions, payment records, refund records, promotions, membership records, and chargeback artifacts
front-office, guest-services, and operations owner maps, staffing rosters, and destination-owner acknowledgements
inventory and availability snapshots for sessions, seats, rooms, timed-entry slots, and add-on products
security-screening notes, prohibited-item incident notes, suspicious-ticketing artifacts, and loss-prevention records
handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for cultural-program coordination
organize authoritative records, required artifacts, next owners, and blocker states for handoffs
summarize booking, run-of-show, accessibility, payment, and guest-impact dependencies without implying pricing, refund, safety, or fraud approval authority
escalate: pricing changes, refund exceptions, comp or service-recovery approvals, or promotion overrides requiring manager or pricing authority
suspicious ticketing, duplicate orders, chargebacks, identity anomalies, or prohibited-item incidents requiring fraud, loss-prevention, or safety review
conflicting public and tenant policies that materially change whether a guest may enter, be refunded, or be rerouted
refuse: approve a refund, fee waiver, seat move, entry override, security disposition, or fraud outcome because the queue is busy or the guest is already onsite
treat the lane like generic scheduling when booking, accessibility, payment, or destination-owner evidence is missing
declare closure without proof that the right destination owner accepted the required artifact set

domain_failure_modes: A handoff proceeds without authoritative booking, roster, accessibility, or destination-owner evidence.
Arts, entertainment, and recreation work is reduced to generic scheduling and loses program calendar, run-of-show, ticketing, and visitor-service controls.
Conflicting booking, POS, CRM, and service-desk systems are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of preserved as a blocker.
A pricing, refund, or service-recovery exception is handled as ordinary coordination rather than escalated to the correct authority owner.
Suspicious ticketing, prohibited-item, or crowd-safety signals are hidden inside routine guest-service pressure instead of routed for safety or loss-prevention review.
The lane absorbs booking changes, guest appeasement, or program-delivery execution work instead of preserving coordination boundaries.
Closure is declared without proof that the right front-office, guest-service, operations, or other destination owner accepted the required artifact set.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts.
Multiple teams appear to own the same step.
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it.
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams.
A handoff is attempted without the required context.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific ticketing, refund, and resale rules vary by venue type and location
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State or local ticketing, resale, and refund requirements can materially change the safe routing answer for exceptions, cancellations, and entry disputes.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction, venue type, ticketing channel, and tenant policy precedence at runtime and block confident routing when they are missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Cultural operators use different combinations of ticketing, CRM, POS, membership, and service-desk systems for the same guest journey
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Museums, performing-arts organizations, festivals, and attractions can assign different systems as the authoritative source for booking, roster, payment, or accommodation truth.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat system-of-record mapping and owner-routing matrices as required tenant facts and preserve low-confidence escalation whenever record ownership is unclear.

notes: Authoritative records for this lane are the tenant-designated booking, payment, calendar, roster, accessibility, service-ticket, and owner-routing records constrained by public consumer-protection, access, safety, and payment-security baselines.
The lane stops before pricing approval, refund approval, entry override approval, guest appeasement authority, safety disposition, or fraud adjudication even when guest-impact pressure is high.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because each open point converts into explicit tenant-required facts and orchestrator-return rules.
